content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Perform the following operation sequence:


For integers i=1,2,...Ni = 1, 2, ...Ni=1,2,...N (in this order), perform one of the following actions:

Choose an integer XXX such that 1‚â§X<N1 \le X < N1‚â§X<N, and either PX=iP_X = iPX‚Äã=i or PX+1=iP_{X + 1} = iPX+1‚Äã=i, then swap PXP_XPX‚Äã and PX+1P_{X + 1}PX+1‚Äã.
Choose to do nothing.


Let f(P)f(P)f(P) denote the lexicographically smallest permutation possible after the above operation sequence.

You are given integers NNN and KKK.

Let III denote the identity permutation of length NNN, i.e. I=[1,2,3,...,N]I = [1, 2, 3, ..., N]I=[1,2,3,...,N].

Consider the sequence of permutations such that f(P)=If(P) = If(P)=I, sorted in lexicographic order. Find the KthK^{th}Kth lexicographically smallest permutation in this sequence.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains NNN and KKK - the parameters defined in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



If there are at least KKK permutations of length NNN such that f(P)=If(P) = If(P)=I, find the KthK^{th}Kth lexicographically smallest one.
otherwise, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 555 permutations of length 333 that have f(P)=If(P) = If(P)=I. In lexicographic order, they are:

P=[1,2,3]P = [1, 2, 3]P=[1,2,3]
P=[1,3,2]P = [1, 3, 2]P=[1,3,2]
P=[2,1,3]P = [2, 1, 3]P=[2,1,3]
P=[2,3,1]P = [2, 3, 1]P=[2,3,1]
P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2]

For example, here is how P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2] can be converted into III:

On the first operation, i=1i = 1i=1, swap P1P_1P1‚Äã and P2P_2P2‚Äã (Note that P2P_2P2‚Äã before the swap is 111 so this is valid). Now, P=[1,3,2]P = [1, 3, 2]P=[1,3,2]
On the second operation, i=2i = 2i=2, choose to do nothing.
On the third operation, i=3i = 3i=3, swap P2P_2P2‚Äã and P3P_3P3‚Äã (Note that P2P_2P2‚Äã before the swap is 222 so this is valid). Now, P=[1,2,3]P = [1, 2, 3]P=[1,2,3]

Thus, we obtained III, and since III is the lexicographically smallest permutation possible, f(P)=If(P) = If(P)=I.
